caption: Figure 1: The Compression Gap. In the continuous pathway (Diffusion Policy), the vision encoder is the binding bottleneck; upgrading it propagates end-to-end. In the discrete pathway (OAT), the codebook (I(Z; T) ≤ 80 bits) is the binding bottleneck; encoder upgrades are blocked at quantization.
description2: The figure plots a comparison between two pathways, "Diffusion Policy" and "OAT", highlighting the binding bottlenecks in each.
description3: Four-panel diagrams compare continuous and discrete pathways, highlighting bottlenecks at the vision encoder and codebook, respectively.
questions: How do the continuous and discrete pathways differ in where the binding bottleneck occurs?
What is the effect of encoder upgrades on the continuous versus discrete pathways?
